setting.note: Rulfo set his town of the dead at the foot of the volcano in Colima, and the map holds the real Comala it grew from: a white-walled plaza, a parish church, the dusty road that drops down from the north, and the fire-mountain on the skyline. Juan Preciado goes looking for the father he was promised and finds a place where only the dead still speak.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Juan Rulfo, first published 1955. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Pedro Páramo, Juan Rulfo (1955). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The ember of the volcano that stands over Comala, banked down to the colour of dried blood and old brick. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
